system.description: Recognize, design, and break secret codes and messages.
system.notes: Study a code for two hours before attempting to break it. A failed roll requires two more hours of study before trying again. Attempting the break after only ten minutes of study carries a -30% penalty.

system.description: Operate sophisticated laser and fiber optic communication systems.
system.notes: Requires Radio: Basic, Electrical Engineer, and Computer Operation.

system.description: Use optical enhancement gear such as telescopic lenses, thermo-imagers, infrared systems, and filters.
system.notes: If the character also knows T.V./Video, add a one-time +5% bonus to that skill.

system.description: Operate field radios, walkie-talkies, wire lines, communication security, and visual signals.
Use masking, scrambling, and unscrambling equipment to protect radio traffic.
Operate and hide surveillance equipment, run stakeouts, and follow subjects without being noticed.
system.notes: A failed roll can mean poor recordings, a bug that fails to transmit, or a tail that has been spotted. Complex high-tech systems also require Computer Operation and Literacy.

system.description: Use video and audio recording equipment, field cameras, studio gear, editing, dubbing, and transmission equipment.
Plan and prepare meals.
system.notes: A failed roll means the food is edible but badly prepared or unpleasant to eat.

system.description: Perform practiced dance steps smoothly and learn new moves quickly.
Catch fish with the proper bait, poles, hooks, and lines, then clean and prepare them for eating.
Play one specific musical instrument with practiced skill.
system.notes: Take this skill once for each separate instrument the character can play.

system.description: Mend clothing, perform small alterations, and cut and sew simple patterns.
system.notes: Taking this skill twice can be treated as tailoring.

system.description: Carry a tune and read music well enough to perform songs.
Understand simple circuits, wiring, schematics, and basic appliance repair.
Diagnose, repair, or sabotage computers and similar hardware.
system.notes: Roll once to figure out the problem and a second time to complete the repair or sabotage.

system.description: Diagnose electrical problems, repair complex devices, wire buildings or vehicles, and build electrical systems.
system.notes: Simple alarms or security systems are at -20%; complex ones are at -50%. Cut those penalties in half if the character also knows Surveillance Systems. Alien or techno-wizard devices are at -30%.

system.description: Repair and understand robotics, military robot electronics, and related systems.
system.notes: Alien or extremely unfamiliar robot electronics are at -40%.

system.description: Spot likely ambush ground and recognize the signs of guerilla tactics.
Spot camouflage, concealed structures or vehicles, and prepare unobtrusive shelters.
Use makeup, wigs, dyes, and putty to alter a person's appearance.
Slip restraints through muscle control, joint work, knot knowledge, and hidden tools.
system.notes: This does not replace Pick Locks.

system.description: Create false documents, signatures, passports, and other printed materials.
system.notes: The forger needs an original or a copy to work from. Skilled forgers can recognize other counterfeits at -10%.

system.description: Assess enemy activity, identify units and leaders, estimate strength, and recognize significant battlefield information.
system.notes: A failed roll means the evidence is inconclusive or the character misreads the situation.

system.description: Open key locks and basic tumbler locks with picks and tools.
system.notes: Each attempt takes 1D6 melee rounds. A failed roll means the lock holds and another attempt is needed.

system.description: Remove small items from a person without being noticed.
system.notes: On a failed roll, the item is not taken and there is a 67% chance the victim notices the attempt.

system.description: Use long-range rifles for aimed single-shot marksmanship.
system.notes: Add +2 to strike on an aimed shot. Only rifles that can fire a single round or blast can be used for sniping.

system.description: Follow the signs left by people, animals, or vehicles and read the trail they leave behind.
system.notes: Roll once every 40 yards or meters while following a trail. Three consecutive failed rolls means the trail is lost.

system.description: Find water, food, shelter, and safe travel practices when stranded in the wild.
Repair, rebuild, modify, and redesign conventional aircraft and hovercraft.
Repair, rebuild, modify, and redesign conventional ground vehicles and their engines.
system.notes: Hover jet systems are at -20%; reactor engines are at -40%.

system.description: Repair, build, modify, and open key, tumbler, combination, and many electrical locks.
system.notes: Older locks may take 1D4 melees; advanced electronic locks may take minutes or hours depending on complexity.

system.description: Analyze, repair, redesign, build, or sabotage machines and mechanical devices.
system.notes: Add a one-time +5% bonus to Locksmith and Surveillance Systems if the character also knows Mechanical Engineer. Alien or techno-wizard mechanics are at -30%.

system.description: Repair, modify, build, and sabotage robots, power armor, and exoskeletons.
system.notes: Alien or extremely unfamiliar robot mechanics are at -30%.

system.description: Maintain, repair, modify, mount, and understand military weapon systems and body armor.
Use basic police science, fingerprinting, ballistics, and forensic medicine to examine evidence and corpses.
Bandage wounds, stop bleeding, administer CPR, and use basic antiseptics and painkillers.
Use plants and natural remedies to prepare medicines, salves, anesthetics, tonics, and poisons.
system.notes: A failed roll means the treatment or concoction does not work. Seasonal availability of plants can limit what can be prepared.

system.description: Perform advanced emergency treatment, set broken bones, suture wounds, use oxygen and emergency gear, and stabilize the badly injured.
system.notes: For coma or severe injury, roll three times. Two successes out of three means the patient is stabilized.

system.description: Diagnose illness, treat patients, perform surgery, and manage general medical care.
system.notes: Use the listed skill for diagnosis. Use 50% +5% per level for treatment and surgery rolls.

system.description: Perform complex surgery and install or remove cybernetic devices.
system.notes: Use the listed skill for diagnosis and non-surgical treatment. Use 60% +5% per level for surgery and cybernetic installation. Bionic work is at -15% unless the character also qualifies as an M.D.B.

system.description: Study disease, tissue damage, abnormal cell structure, and laboratory research methods.
Set explosives, place charges, and sabotage structures or equipment.
system.notes: A failed roll means a dud with no explosion.

system.description: Safely defuse mines, bombs, booby traps, dud rounds, and other explosive devices.
Pilot propeller aircraft with one or two engines.
Drive cars, jeeps, dune buggies, and small trucks.
Pilot small to medium sailing craft and fishing boats.
Pilot small motor-driven boats, yachts, and hydrofoils.
Pilot large seafaring sailing ships and major watercraft.
system.notes: Use 44% +4% per level for motor-driven ships.

system.description: Pilot helicopters, including combat helicopters.
system.notes: Weapon use on combat helicopters still requires Weapon Systems.

system.description: Ride, care for, judge, and maneuver horses in travel or combat.
system.notes: While charging on horseback, gain +1 to parry, +1 to dodge, and +4 damage. Projectile fire from a moving horse is inaccurate, and aimed shots are impossible.

system.description: Pilot hover cycles and ground-effect hover vehicles.
Pilot commercial jets and similar aircraft.
Pilot fighter aircraft and use fighter tactics in the air.
Pilot backpack jet units and maintain airborne control close to the ground.
Pilot motorcycles, dirt bikes, mopeds, and other two- or three-wheeled vehicles.
Pilot giant robots, robot vehicles, and power armor suits.
system.notes: This covers piloting only. Combat use is handled through Robot Combat skills.

system.description: Fight with standard robots and power armor at the basic training level.
system.notes: Use the Basic Robot Combat Training tables from the combat section for attacks, bonuses, and handling.

system.description: Master one specific robot or power armor type and fight with it at peak efficiency.
system.notes: Select this skill separately for each robot type. Use the robot combat tables in the combat section for the exact bonuses and attacks.

system.description: Pilot large cargo and transport trucks.
Pilot tanks, armored personnel carriers, and other heavy military vehicles.
Read maps, charts, landmarks, and instruments to stay on course by land, air, or sea.
system.notes: On a failed roll, the pilot is off course. Determine distance off course by the vehicle type being used.

system.description: Read and operate radar, sonar, sensor panels, detection gear, and other vehicle or surveillance instruments.
Operate vehicle, robot, and power armor weapon systems such as missiles, cannons, rail guns, and lasers.
system.notes: Add +1 to strike when using these mounted or integrated weapon systems. Hand-held weapons are covered by weapon proficiencies.

system.description: Break into computer systems, bypass defenses, and steal or sabotage data.
system.notes: If the character is a hacker, add a one-time +5% bonus to Cryptography, Surveillance Systems, and Locksmith when dealing with electronic or computer-controlled locks.

system.description: Hide small objects on the body or in hand without being noticed.
system.notes: Small and light objects are easier to hide. Larger objects must still fit the size and weight limits described in the skill.

system.description: Make a small object seem to disappear into the hand during close work.
system.notes: Add a +5% bonus to Pick Pockets.

system.description: Recognize gangs, black-market operators, criminal hangouts, and dangerous city situations.
